facts.claim: Table patient_cache has 11 columns: mpi_id VARCHAR(64), tenant_id VARCHAR(32), display_name VARCHAR(255), mrn VARCHAR(64), gender VARCHAR(16), birth_date DATE, admission_dt TIMESTAMPTZ, bed_id VARCHAR(32), unit VARCHAR(64), synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ, is_active BOOLEAN

Table vital_sign has 13 columns: id BIGSERIAL, mpi_id VARCHAR(64), recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ, heart_rate INT, systolic_bp INT, diastolic_bp INT, temperature DECIMAL(4,1), spo2 INT, respiratory_rate INT, avpu VARCHAR(4), supplemental_o2 BOOLEAN, source_system VARCHAR(32), ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ

Table clinical_score has 9 columns: id BIGSERIAL, mpi_id VARCHAR(64), score_type VARCHAR(16), score_value INT, calculated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, vital_sign_id BIGINT, components JSONB, trend VARCHAR(16), delta_from_previous INT

Table alert has 14 columns: id BIGSERIAL, mpi_id VARCHAR(64), score_id BIGINT, severity VARCHAR(16), status VARCHAR(16), title VARCHAR(255), body TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ, acknowledged_by VARCHAR(255), resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolution VARCHAR(32)

Table threshold_config has 12 columns: id SERIAL, tenant_id VARCHAR(32), unit VARCHAR(64), score_type VARCHAR(16), watch_threshold INT, urgent_threshold INT, critical_threshold INT, rate_limit_per_hour INT, cooldown_minutes INT, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_by VARCHAR(255)
vital_sign is a TimescaleDB hypertable with time dimension recorded_at and space partitioning by mpi_id; creation DDL: SELECT create_hypertable('vital_sign', 'recorded_at');
clinical_score is a TimescaleDB hypertable with time dimension calculated_at and space partitioning by mpi_id; creation DDL: SELECT create_hypertable('clinical_score', 'calculated_at');
alert is a TimescaleDB hypertable with time dimension created_at and space partitioning by mpi_id; creation DDL: SELECT create_hypertable('alert', 'created_at');
vital_sign retention policy: 90 days; justification: dados brutos mantidos para auditoria clínica de curto prazo
clinical_score retention policy: 7 years; justification: scores históricos para análise de qualidade e pesquisa
alert retention policy: 7 years; justification: registro de alertas para compliance e melhoria do sistema
patient_cache retention policy: até alta + 30 dias (until discharge + 30 days); justification: cache volátil; fonte primária é o MPI
Patient demographics data resides in MPI (mpi.patients master_patient_id) at AMH Data Platform; direction: read into patient_cache local cache
facts.source_ref: model.md §Relação com a AMH Data Platform

facts.claim: Vital signs data resides in AMH Gold layer (Apache Iceberg); direction: read into vital_sign via Athena query
facts.source_ref: model.md §Relação com a AMH Data Platform

facts.claim: Clinical scores computed locally in clinical_score table; direction: write periodically to AMH Gold layer table fact_patient_score
facts.source_ref: model.md §Relação com a AMH Data Platform

facts.claim: Alerts generated locally in alert table; direction: write periodically to AMH Gold layer table fact_alert
facts.source_ref: model.md §Relação com a AMH Data Platform

facts.claim: Thresholds stored locally in threshold_config table; direction: local only, no AMH Gold layer persistence
facts.source_ref: model.md §Relação com a AMH Data Platform

facts.claim: Conceptual ER diagram uses entity names: Patient, VitalSign, Score, Alert; DDL table names differ: patient_cache, vital_sign, clinical_score, alert
vital_sign table primary key: id BIGSERIAL
clinical_score table primary key: id BIGSERIAL
alert table primary key: id BIGSERIAL
threshold_config table primary key: id SERIAL
patient_cache.mpi_id is FK to mpi.patients.master_patient_id at AMH Data Platform MPI
vital_sign.mpi_id is FK to patient_cache.mpi_id
clinical_score.mpi_id is FK to patient_cache.mpi_id
clinical_score.vital_sign_id is FK to vital_sign.id
alert.mpi_id is FK to patient_cache.mpi_id
alert.score_id is FK to clinical_score.id
heart_rate measured in bpm (beats per minute); column type INT in vital_sign
systolic_bp and diastolic_bp measured in mmHg (millimeters of mercury); column type INT in vital_sign
temperature measured in °C (degrees Celsius); column type DECIMAL(4,1) in vital_sign
spo2 (oxygen saturation) measured in % (percent); column type INT in vital_sign
respiratory_rate measured in rpm (respirations per minute); column type INT in vital_sign
avpu (Alert/Verbal/Pain/Unresponsive) consciousness level enumeration with values A, V, P, U; column type VARCHAR(4) in vital_sign
score_type enumeration: MEWS, NEWS2, SOFA, qSOFA; column type VARCHAR(16) in clinical_score
severity enumeration: watch, urgent, critical; column type VARCHAR(16) in alert
status enumeration: active, acknowledged, resolved, expired; column type VARCHAR(16) in alert
resolution enumeration: true_positive, false_positive, intervention_done; column type VARCHAR(32) in alert
trend enumeration: increasing, decreasing, stable vs anterior score; column type VARCHAR(16) in clinical_score
Dados analíticos de longo prazo residem no Gold layer da AMH Data Platform (Apache Iceberg) com time-travel 30 dias e snapshots
Database system: PostgreSQL with TimescaleDB extension for time-series data
Operational data model resides in PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB; analytical data in AMH Data Platform (Apache Iceberg)
clinical_score.components is JSONB column storing score subcomponents as key-value pairs (e.g., respiratory_rate: 2, spo2: 1, ...)
vital_sign.source_system column tracks origin system (e.g., tasy, philips_monitor); column type VARCHAR(32)

constraints.text: MUST preserve PT-BR clinical vocabulary verbatim with accents in all descriptions and comments sourced from legacy catalog

MUST maintain referential integrity: vital_sign.mpi_id → patient_cache.mpi_id, clinical_score.mpi_id → patient_cache.mpi_id, clinical_score.vital_sign_id → vital_sign.id, alert.mpi_id → patient_cache.mpi_id, alert.score_id → clinical_score.id
vital_sign table MUST be implemented as TimescaleDB hypertable with time dimension recorded_at and space partitioning by mpi_id
clinical_score table MUST be implemented as TimescaleDB hypertable with time dimension calculated_at and space partitioning by mpi_id
alert table MUST be implemented as TimescaleDB hypertable with time dimension created_at and space partitioning by mpi_id
MUST enforce retention policy: vital_sign data deleted after 90 days; clinical_score and alert data retained for 7 years; patient_cache volatile cache flushed after patient discharge + 30 days
patient_cache is authoritative local cache only — source of truth for patient demographics is MPI (mpi.patients) at AMH Data Platform; synced_at timestamp MUST track last sync
constraints.source_ref: model.md §patient_cache + §Relação com a AMH Data Platform

constraints.text: MUST source vital signs from AMH Gold layer via Athena query; vital_sign.ingested_at MUST differentiate ingest timestamp from vital_sign.recorded_at (clinical collection time)
constraints.source_ref: model.md §vital_sign + §Relação com a AMH Data Platform

constraints.text: MUST write clinical_score records periodically to AMH Gold layer fact_patient_score table for analytics
constraints.source_ref: model.md §Relação com a AMH Data Platform

constraints.text: MUST write alert records periodically to AMH Gold layer fact_alert table for compliance and analytics
constraints.source_ref: model.md §Relação com a AMH Data Platform

constraints.text: threshold_config MUST be stored locally only; no AMH Gold layer persistence; supports tenant and unit granularity (unit can be NULL for tenant-wide defaults)
constraints.source_ref: model.md §threshold_config + §Relação com a AMH Data Platform

constraints.text: MUST maintain mapping between ER diagram entity names (Patient, VitalSign, Score, Alert) and DDL table names (patient_cache, vital_sign, clinical_score, alert) in all documentation
patient_cache.mpi_id MUST be VARCHAR(64) to align with AMH MPI system identifier width
vital_sign.temperature MUST use DECIMAL(4,1) precision to preserve one decimal place (e.g., 37.5°C)
MUST support multi-tenancy via tenant_id (VARCHAR(32)) in patient_cache and threshold_config; tenant_id MUST be indexed for query performance

open_questions: Does patient_cache require a PRIMARY KEY definition, or is mpi_id alone sufficient as natural key?
What is the exact DDL for indexes on hypertables (e.g., space-time indexes on (mpi_id, recorded_at))?
What is the AMH Athena query pattern for ingesting vital signs into vital_sign table — pull-on-demand or streaming CDC?
What is the SLA for periodic writes to AMH Gold layer (fact_patient_score, fact_alert) — real-time, hourly, or daily batches?
Does threshold_config.unit support wildcards or patterns for unit matching, or only exact strings and NULL?
What is the backup/recovery strategy for the 7-year clinical_score and alert retention windows?
